topIssues: Excessive use of borders (chrome) on almost every clickable element (prompts, lenses, modifiers) creates visual clutter.
The 'Axiarete' example card is disproportionately large and mostly empty, wasting valuable screen real estate.
Low contrast on the 'Ask' button text against its background makes the primary CTA feel muted.
Visual competition between the primary search bar and the heavy example cards below the fold.
Cluttered input area with 4 modifier buttons, 3 suggested prompts, and 6 lens toggles all competing for attention right below the main input.
recommendations: Remove borders from suggested prompts and lens toggles; use typography, spacing, or subtle background fills to indicate interactivity.
Shrink the 'Start with one example' card to match the height of the 'More starting points' cards, eliminating the massive empty space.
Convert the input modifiers (Upload, Paste, Voice, Camera) into simple icon buttons inside the search bar to reduce visual noise.
Increase the text contrast of the 'Ask' button to ensure it stands out as the primary CTA.
Group the 'Lens' selection into a single dropdown or a more subtle toggle to reduce the number of distinct UI elements on the screen.

topIssues: Excessive Chrome: Every element is wrapped in a bordered box, creating massive visual clutter and making the UI feel like a wireframe.
Competing Actions: The primary action (chat input) is drowned out by 10+ pills, 4 pending boxes, and 3 columns of empty states.
Premature UI: Showing 'PENDING' boxes for 'What it is', 'Why it matters', etc., before a question is even asked creates unnecessary cognitive load.
Dashed Empty States: The dashed borders in the right column draw too much attention to areas that currently have no value.
Cluttered Input Area: Placing 4 action pills above and 6 persona pills below the input box makes the core interaction zone feel cramped.
recommendations: Remove all borders from the 'PENDING' answer boxes and right-column empty states; use whitespace and typography for hierarchy instead.
Hide the 4 specific answer sections ('What it is', etc.) until the AI actually starts generating content for them.
Consolidate the 6 persona pills (Founder, Investor, etc.) into a single dropdown or toggle near the input to reduce clutter.
Dim or hide the left and right columns entirely on the initial 'Start a live session' screen, revealing them only when context or sources are actually populated.
Center and enlarge the chat input box to make it the undeniable primary action on the screen, similar to ChatGPT or Perplexity.

topIssues: Excessive use of bordered boxes in the right sidebar and middle cards creates visual clutter and a rigid feel.
The 3-pane layout splits user attention, lacking a single dominant focal point for the user's primary job.
Nested micro-typography and inner boxes inside the middle cards are difficult to read and add unnecessary cognitive load.
The 'Memory Loop' educational box in the bottom left takes up valuable screen real estate for a static message.
Lack of visible AI reasoning or inline source links in the actual report content (right pane) reduces trust.
recommendations: Remove the borders around the individual sections ('WHAT IT IS', 'WHY IT MATTERS') in the right sidebar, using typography and whitespace for hierarchy instead.
Simplify the middle cards by removing the nested 'WHAT IT IS / STARTER' sub-boxes and mini-bars, keeping just the title, description, and metadata tags.
Remove the 'Memory Loop' box from the persistent sidebar and integrate its educational message into an empty state or a dismissible tooltip.
Consolidate the layout by potentially hiding the left sidebar behind a toggle or integrating search directly above the middle pane to focus attention on the reports.
Add inline citations or source links to the report content in the right pane to build trust in the AI's synthesis.

topIssues: Excessive use of borders and bounding boxes creates visual clutter and violates chrome collapse.
Mixing educational onboarding content ('What one real loop should look like', 'Next Action Loop' steps) with functional product UI.
Multiple competing primary CTAs ('Open in Chat') across the left and right columns.
Presence of a 'Sign in' button on a surface that requires authentication (personalized nudges).
Redundant 'EXAMPLE' tags on feed items add noise without providing user value.
recommendations: Remove the borders around the hero section, feed cards, and right-panel steps; use whitespace and typography for hierarchy.
Remove the educational 'Next Action Loop' breakdown from the permanent UI and replace it with a focused preview of the selected nudge.
Consolidate the primary action: make the feed items selectable (active state) and keep only one primary 'Open in Chat' CTA in the right-hand detail pane.
Remove the 'Sign in' button and replace it with a user avatar or profile menu, assuming this is an authenticated state.
Remove the 'EXAMPLE' tags and instructional first card ('Do not build a broad...') to make the feed reflect actual user data.

topIssues: Excessive use of bordered boxes (14+ containers) creates visual clutter and violates the chrome collapse principle.
Lack of a single dominant action; the user is presented with a wall of empty states and text to read instead of a guided flow.
Confusing authentication state: The nav shows a 'Sign in' button while the user is viewing a personalized 'Me' profile.
The 'Start in Chat' primary CTA is buried at the bottom left of the screen instead of being a focal point.
The action prompts ('Add one file...') look like static text boxes rather than interactive elements.
recommendations: Remove the borders from the lists on the right and the profile sections in the middle, using whitespace and typography for hierarchy instead.
Consolidate the 'What improves the next run' section into a single, clear onboarding checklist with one primary CTA.
Remove the 'Sign in' button from the navigation if the user is already in an authenticated state, replacing it with an avatar or account menu.
Make the profile sections ('Background summary', etc.) inline-editable text areas rather than static cards that require a click to open a modal.
Remove the massive border around the hero section ('Private context...') to let the header breathe and reduce visual weight.
